'''Junior Moors''' (born 30 July 1986) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr|sr|lf}} for [[Campbelltown City Kangaroos]] in the [[Group 6 Rugby League]].

He previously played for the [[Penrith Panthers]], [[Wests Tigers]] and the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], as well as [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League|Betfred Super League]].

==Background==
Moors was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]], and moved to Australia and was educated at [[Patrician Brothers' College, Blacktown]].

Moors played his junior football for [[Cambridge Park RLFC]] before being signed by the [[Penrith Panthers]]. He played for the Panthers' [[NSW Cup|Premier League]] reserve-grade team in 2006.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com//db/player/m/moors_junior/index.php Player. Moors Junior]rleague.com {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080420053946/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/m/moors_junior/index.php |date=20 April 2008 }}</ref>

==Club career==

=== Penrith Panthers ===
In Round 1 of the [[2007 NRL season]] he made his [[NRL]] debut for the Panthers against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].

In a 2009 pre-season trial, Moors suffered a broken ankle which stopped him from playing first-grade that season.{{cn|date=January 2023}}

=== Wests Tigers ===
In September 2009, Moors signed a one-year contract with the [[Wests Tigers]] starting in 2010, with the Tigers having an option to extend his contract for a further season.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Daily Telegraph (Australia)|title=Daine Laurie a Panther, Tigers on signing spree|author=Todd Balym|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/daine-laurie-a-panther-tigers-on-signing-spree/story-e6frexnr-1225778823667|access-date=24 September 2009|date=23 September 2009}}</ref>

Moors became a regular interchange player in the second half of 2012, making 14 appearances for the year. He said, "It’s something I have struggled with over the last couple of years, keeping my weight down but it’s been good this year. I’m starting to feel more comfortable in the team now."<ref>{{cite news|author=Nathan Ryan |work=Sports News First |title=Tigers cub stalking new deal |url=http://www.sportsnewsfirst.com.au/articles/2012/08/13/tigers-cub-stalking-new-deal/ |access-date=13 September 2012 |date=13 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120817041530/http://www.sportsnewsfirst.com.au/articles/2012/08/13/tigers-cub-stalking-new-deal/ |archive-date=17 August 2012 }}</ref> Statistically, Moors was revealed to be one of the most effective tacklers in the [[NRL]] in 2012, with a 94.1% success rate in tackles.<ref>{{cite news|work=NRL.com |title=Revealed: the NRL's forward defensive heroes |url=http://www.nrl.com/revealed-the-nrls-forward-defensive-heroes/tabid/11037/newsid/68299/default.aspx |access-date=13 September 2012 |date=28 June 2012 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121028035700/http://www.nrl.com/revealed-the-nrls-forward-defensive-heroes/tabid/11037/newsid/68299/default.aspx |archive-date=28 October 2012 }}</ref> He was also a member of the [[Balmain Ryde Eastwood Tigers]] side that lost the [[NSW Cup]] grand final.<ref>{{cite web|author=Wayne Cousins |work=weststigers.com.au |title=Tigers go down fighting to Jets in NSW Cup Grand Final |url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Tigers%20go%20down%20fighting%20to%20Jets%20in%20NSW%20Cup%20Grand%20Final/64669 |access-date=10 November 2012 |date=30 September 2012 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131228214947/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Tigers%20go%20down%20fighting%20to%20Jets%20in%20NSW%20Cup%20Grand%20Final/64669 |archive-date=28 December 2013 }}</ref>

=== Melbourne Storm ===
On 13 September 2012, Moors signed a one-year contract with [[Melbourne Storm]] starting in 2013.<ref>{{cite web|title=Storm builds strong front|url=http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news-display/Storm-builds-strong-front/63919|access-date=2012-09-13|publisher=Melbourne Storm official website|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120916104304/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news-display/Storm-builds-strong-front/63919|archive-date=2012-09-16}}</ref>

His first game was interchange for Storm's [[2013 World Club Challenge]] win over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

===Castleford Tigers===
On 11 June 2014, it was confirmed that Moors had signed a three-year contract to play for the [[Super League]] club the [[Castleford Tigers]] ([[List of Castleford Tigers players|Heritage No. 953]]) from 2015.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2014-06-11|title=Tigers Sign Samoan International Junior Moors|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=3292|access-date=2020-06-05|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> Castleford head coach [[Daryl Powell]] said of the signing: "I think his style is going to be really well-suited to the Super League and he'll be a real threat."<ref>{{Cite news|date=2014-06-11|title=Castleford Tigers sign Melbourne Storm forward Junior Moors|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27794248|access-date=2020-06-05}}</ref>

Moors was given squad number 16 ahead of his first season with the Tigers. Moors made his Super League début against the [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the first round, on 8 February 2015.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2015-02-08|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 22-24 Wakefield Trinity Wildcats|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/31113016|access-date=2020-06-05}}</ref> He scored his first try for the club against the [[Warrington Wolves]] in round 23, on 26 July.<ref>{{Cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|date=2015-07-26|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 6-44 Warrington Wolves|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/33610038|access-date=2020-06-05}}</ref> He finished the year having made 25 appearances and scored 4 tries.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Junior Moors - Career Stats & Summary|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/junior-moors/summary.html|access-date=2020-06-05|website=Rugby League Project}}</ref>

For 2016, Moors was assigned shirt number 17. He suffered a [[broken hand]] in March, missing 8 games, although returned earlier than expected - Daryl Powell described his post-injury form as "outstanding", and called him "a tough character".<ref>{{Cite web|date=2016-05-13|title=Castleford's Junior Moors defied medical advice to inspire big win at Leeds|url=https://www.givemesport.com/775829-castlefords-junior-moors-defied-medical-advice-to-inspire-big-win-at-leeds|access-date=2020-06-05|website=GiveMeSport|language=en-GB}}</ref> His "irrepressible" performance in Castleford's 52–12 victory over West Yorkshire rivals [[Leeds Rhinos]] saw him voted as the Fans' Man of the Match.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2016-05-12|title=Leeds Rhinos 12 Castleford Tigers 52 - Luke Dorn dominates as Cas crush sorry Leeds|work=The Yorkshire Post|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-12-castleford-tigers-52-luke-dorn-dominates-cas-crush-sorry-leeds-1798807#gsc.tab=0|access-date=2020-06-05}}</ref> His outstanding personal year saw him dominate the club's end-of-season awards, being recognised as the Fans' Player of the Year, Players' Player of the Year, and 1st Place Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2016-09-21|title=Moors scoops hat-trick of player of the year awards|work=Pontefract & Castleford Express|url=https://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/moors-scoops-hat-trick-player-year-awards-2131405#gsc.tab=0|access-date=2020-06-05}}</ref> In October, he signed a two-year contract extension with the Tigers until the end of 2019.<ref name=":1">{{Cite news|date=2016-10-13|title=Junior Moors: Castleford Tigers forward signs new contract|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37647240|access-date=2020-06-05}}</ref> "He had an enormous 2016, being one of the best forwards in Super League," said Daryl Powell.<ref name=":1" />

Moors was utilised as an impact player for the 2017 season, making the vast majority of his appearances as an interchange.<ref name=":0" /> In June, he sustained a knee ligament injury which kept him out for 8 games.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2017-06-15|title=Injury Update|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5522|access-date=2020-06-05|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en}}</ref> He continued his impressive performances, and greatly contributed towards the Tigers 1st-placed finish in Super League. Resultantly, in August he signed a further one-year contract extension, keeping him at Castleford until the end of 2020.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|date=2017-08-10|title=Junior Moors signs Castleford extension|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/50734/junior-moors-signs-castleford-extension|access-date=2020-06-05|website=www.rugby-league.com}}</ref> Daryl Powell said, "Junior Moors is a high-quality player who has continued to get better during his time at the club."<ref name=":2" /> He played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=7 October 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=Guardian|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

Following the retirement of [[Andy Lynch (rugby league)|Andy Lynch]], Moors was given squad number 8 for 2018.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2018-01-23|title=2018 Squad Numbers|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5773|access-date=2020-06-05|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en}}</ref> This, however, didn't reflect any change in his role on the pitch, as he remained a key impact substitute.<ref name=":0" /> He largely avoided any long-term injuries and made 25 appearances this year. He also scored 8 tries, including his first brace against the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on 13 September - this made the 2018 season his highest-scoring campaign yet.<ref name=":0" />

Moors' 2019 season was severely limited by serious injuries - he sustained a knee injury in April, keeping him out for 2 months, before suffering a season-ending hip fracture in just his second game back.<ref>{{Cite news|last=Harber|first=Tony|date=2019-06-25|title=Castleford Tigers star Junior Moors suffers fractured hip in Salford game|work=Pontefract & Castleford Express|url=https://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-star-junior-moors-suffers-fractured-hip-salford-game-687186#gsc.tab=0|access-date=2020-06-05}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-08-24|title=First Team Injury Update|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6547|access-date=2020-06-05|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en}}</ref> He made 13 appearances and scored 3 tries.<ref name=":0" />{{reflist|group=lower-alpha}}

In November 2020, Castleford coach [[Daryl Powell]] confirmed the departure of Moors.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-confirm-departure-of-junior-moors/|title=Castleford confirm departure of Junior Moors|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=10 November 2020|access-date=14 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 20 January 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Jan 2021|access-date=21 Jan 2021|title=Featherstone snap up Junior Moors|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/championship/}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Moors played for Featherstone in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final loss against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk|date=29 May 2022 }}</ref>
On 8 November 2023, Moors announced his retirement from professional rugby league<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Nov 2023|title=Former Castleford Tigers, Featherstone Rovers powerhouse retires after 17-year professional career: 'The time has come'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-castleford-tigers-featherstone-rovers-powerhouse-junior-moors-retires-after-17-year-professional-career}}</ref>

==International career==
While attending [[Patrician Brothers' College Blacktown]], Moors played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 2004.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2013, Junior was selected for the [[Samoa national rugby league team]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]. He played in 3 of the Samoans 4 tournament games, scoring one try against the [[France national rugby league team|French]] in the group stage.{{cn|date=January 2023}}
